title: thymus serpyllum subsp. montanus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. montanus is a subspecies of the wild thyme plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its small, pink or purple flowers and strong, pungent aroma. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. montanus include its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve respiratory function, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, coughs, and flu. Thymus serpyllum subsp. montanus can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or dried herb for use in herbal remedies, and is also used in culinary dishes to add flavor and aroma.
